title: 1866 will of John A. Dible, Westmoreland County, Pennsylvania
repository: Westmoreland County Register of Wills; accessed through Ancestry
citation: Westmoreland County, Pennsylvania, Will Books, volumes 3-5, 1839-1870, case 2663, will of John A. Dible, dated 15 December 1866 and proved 1 January 1867, pages 105-106; Ancestry collection 8802, record 1834758.
transcription: John A. Dible of Penn Township names wife Rebecah and children including Zephaniah in his will. Jacob and Franklin Dible were appointed executors. The indexed relationship and document place Zephaniah among the testator's children, but do not by themselves establish birth order.

title: Catherine Moore Dible household and parentage records
repository: United States census and Westmoreland County marriage records accessed through FamilySearch; 1901 Wapello County history
citation: 1860 U.S. census, Allegheny Township, Armstrong County, Pennsylvania, schedule 1, page 48, dwelling 747, family 744, lines 29-31, Zephaniah and Catherine Dible household. Westmoreland County, Pennsylvania, marriage application 628, pages 252-253, Mary E. Dible, 11 November 1886. Samuel B. Evans, ed., History of Wapello County, Iowa, and Representative Citizens (1901), page 559.
transcription: The 1860 census enumerates Catherine Dible, age 21 and Pennsylvania-born, with Zephaniah Dible in Allegheny Township, supporting birth about 1839 and proving she was alive on 12 July 1860. In 1886, daughter Mary E. Dible signed a marriage application naming her parents as Zephaniah and Catherine Dible. A 1901 biographical sketch of son John calls him a son of Zephaniah and Catherine Moore Dible and says Zephaniah later remarried Clarinda Stodghill. These records establish Catherine's identity as wife and mother but do not prove her parents, death year, or burial; the unsourced 1864 death and conflicting 1870 or 1873 remarriage dates remain unresolved.

title: 1850 and 1860 census households of A. J. and Sophia Haden
repository: National Archives and Records Administration, Record Group 29; accessed through Ancestry
citation: 1850 U.S. census, Cumberland County, Kentucky, dwelling 823, family 823, household of A. J. Haden, line 30; Ancestry collection 8054, record 17025972. 1860 U.S. census, District 1, Cumberland County, Kentucky, dwelling 389, family 389, A. J. Haden and Robert Ried entries; Ancestry collection 7667, records 41576084 and 41576264.
transcription: The 1850 index identifies A. J. Haden, age 34, born about 1816 in Virginia, in Cumberland County dwelling and family 823. The extracted household sequence names Sophia Hayden, age 33, followed by children or younger residents including M. R., S. A., E., James W., and Robert R.; the sequence assigns E. an age of about eight, consistent with Elizabeth J. Haden's circa-1843 birth. The cockpit extraction shifts some household cells because the 1850 schedule has no relationship column, so the order and ages are retained cautiously rather than silently normalized. The 1860 index identifies A. J. Haden, age 45, born in Virginia, and Sophia Haden, age 43, in District 1, Cumberland County, dwelling and family 389. A separately indexed Robert Ried, age 69, born in Kentucky, appears in that exact same dwelling and family. The repeated couple, county, ages, and birthplaces independently link the 1850 and 1860 households. Co-residence strongly supports the proposed Haden family placement and confirms the older Reid/Ried associate, but neither census states that E. was a daughter or that Robert was Sophia's father; those relationships remain inferential pending marriage, probate, deed, Bible, or other direct evidence.
